elements.title: Hadley Williams
elements.contents: ... that doctor Hadley Williams (pictured) left London for London, then returned to London?

elements.title: Kulembebe
elements.contents: ... that the kingdom of Kulembebe in modern-day Angola may have been founded in the 13th century?

elements.title: killing a child
elements.contents: ... that a television station mistakenly broadcast footage of a different person three times while trying to film a suspect for killing a child?

elements.title: Jake West
elements.contents: ... that Northwestern basketball coach Chris Collins claimed that he did not know his player Jake West was an influencer until Collins's own daughter told him?

elements.title: Syriac Assembly Movement
elements.contents: ... that prominent anti-Islam protester Salwan Momika was a key figure for the Syriac Assembly Movement during the 2018 Iraqi parliamentary election, despite living in Sweden at the time?

elements.title: Natasha Pickowicz
elements.contents: ... that New York–based pastry chef Natasha Pickowicz wrote a cookbook on Chinese hot pot?

elements.title: Ōtari-Wilton's Bush
elements.contents: ... that Ōtari-Wilton's Bush is the only public botanic garden in New Zealand that is entirely dedicated to native plants?

elements.title: 1965 Michigan State Spartans football team
elements.contents: ... that eight players from the national-champion 1965 Michigan State Spartans football team, including MVP Steve Juday, received first-team All-America honors?

elements.title: Saint Sadalberga
elements.contents: ... that Saint Sadalberga used telepathy to "order lettuce from her gardener", according to a story from her vita?
